TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr_domestic_troubles_Muu
TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @article{sung2008developing,title={Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages.},author={Sung, Li-May and Lily, I and Hsieh, Fuhui and Lin, Zhemin and others},journal={Taiwan Journal of Linguistics},volume={6},number={2},year={2008}}

S.FORM: sua sua iku masin ia sua nganaraku ia Mu'u kis Č Čn Kanakanavu.
S.PHON: sua sua iku masin ia sua Ňčanar~…ĺaku ia mu Ēu kis…®…®n kanakanavu.
S.FORM: sua sua iku masin ia sua nganaraku ia Mu'u kis Č Čn Kanakanavu.
S.PHON: sua sua iku masin ia sua Ňčanar~…ĺaku ia mu Ēu kis…®…®n kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: Now I, my name is called Mu'u in Kanakanavu.

S.FORM: sua teku poisua sua makai tanasamia tanasamaku sii marangcikia.
S.PHON: sua teku poisua sua makai tanasamia tanasamaku sii mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikia.
S.FORM: sua teku poisua sua makai tanasamia tanasamaku sii marangcikia.
S.PHON: sua teku poisua sua makai tanasamia tanasamaku sii mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikia.
S.TRANSL: I will talk about...like my home, because I become old.

S.FORM: cucuru kan 'unakia manu nakai 'esi namanamar Č.
S.PHON: ¶u ¶ur~…ĺu kan Ēunakia manu nakai Ēesi namanamar~…ĺ…®.
S.FORM: cucuru kan 'unakia manu nakai 'esi namanamar Č.
S.PHON: ¶u ¶ur~…ĺu kan Ēunakia manu nakai Ēesi namanamar~…ĺ…®.
S.TRANSL: To be frank, I have children, but they are in the flatland.

S.FORM: 'esi namanamar Č nakai sua nguan sua mamanumaku ia 'esi takacicin kavavantuku.
S.PHON: Ēesi namanamar~…ĺ…® nakai sua Ňčuan sua mamanumaku ia Ēesi taka ¶i ¶in kavavantuku.
S.FORM: 'esi namanamar Č nakai sua nguan sua mamanumaku ia 'esi takacicin kavavantuku.
S.PHON: Ēesi namanamar~…ĺ…® nakai sua Ňčuan sua mamanumaku ia Ēesi taka ¶i ¶in kavavantuku.

TRANSL.notes: (They are) in the flatland, but they, my children, are making money by themselves.

S.TRANSL: in the flatland, but they, my children, are making money by themselves.

S.FORM: ka'an er ka'an tavar Č' Č movua mamarang pirati'ing sii 'una kavangvang tating.
S.PHON: ka Ēan er~…ĺ ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® movua mamar~…ĺaŇč pir~…ĺati ĒiŇč sii Ēuna kavaŇčvaŇč tatiŇč.
S.FORM: ka'an er ka'an tavar Č' Č movua mamarang pirati'ing sii 'una kavangvang tating.
S.PHON: ka Ēan er~…ĺ ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® movua mamar~…ĺaŇč pir~…ĺati ĒiŇč sii Ēuna kavaŇčvaŇč tatiŇč.

TRANSL.notes: (They) cannot give the elderly a little money, because (they) all have children.

S.TRANSL: cannot give the elderly a little money, because all have children.

S.FORM: 'una neci'ire isua ikim masin mata er miciku ia marangcikim.
S.PHON: Ēuna ne ¶i Ēir~…ĺe isua ikim masin mata er~…ĺ mi ¶iku ia mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikim.
S.FORM: 'una neci'ire isua ikim masin mata er miciku ia marangcikim.
S.PHON: Ēuna ne ¶i Ēir~…ĺe isua ikim masin mata er~…ĺ mi ¶iku ia mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikim.
S.TRANSL: See, now I and Miciku get old.

S.FORM: tassa marangcikimi cucuru 'ecikia mamia kan aka paira maa c Čp Čngaku si tecikiae kusa makenanu?
S.PHON: tassa mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikimi ¶u ¶ur~…ĺu Ēe ¶ikia mamia kan aka pair~…ĺa maa ¶…®p…®Ňčaku si te ¶ikiae kusa makenanu?
S.FORM: tassa marangcikimi cucuru 'ecikia mamia kan aka paira maa c Čp Čngaku si tecikiae kusa makenanu?
S.PHON: tassa mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikimi ¶u ¶ur~…ĺu Ēe ¶ikia mamia kan aka pair~…ĺa maa ¶…®p…®Ňčaku si te ¶ikiae kusa makenanu?

TRANSL.notes: Both of us get old, and I keep being upset often. So what should I do (with that)?

S.TRANSL: Both of us get old, and I keep being upset often. So what should I do ?

S.FORM: tecikia t Čv Čt Čv Čt Čv Čs Č mamamane.
S.PHON: te ¶ikia t…®v…®t…®v…®t…®v…®s…® mamamane.
S.FORM: tecikia t Čv Čt Čv Čt Čv Čs Č mamamane.
S.PHON: te ¶ikia t…®v…®t…®v…®t…®v…®s…® mamamane.

W.FORM: t Čv Č-t Čv Č-t Čv Čs Č
t Čv Č-t Čv Č-t Čv Čs Č

S.FORM: necire kusa tia cumac Č' Čra mamanu misaaku mamia pac Čp Čc Čp Čng.
S.PHON: ne ¶ir~…ĺe kusa tia ¶uma ¶…® Ē…®r~…ĺa mamanu misaaku mamia pa ¶…®p…® ¶…®p…®Ňč.
S.FORM: necire kusa tia cumac Č' Čra mamanu misaaku mamia pac Čp Čc Čp Čng.
S.PHON: ne ¶ir~…ĺe kusa tia ¶uma ¶…® Ē…®r~…ĺa mamanu misaaku mamia pa ¶…®p…® ¶…®p…®Ňč.
S.TRANSL: I keep thinking about who on earth is going to take care of my child.

S.FORM: apaco'e nuu monakuin na 'umo'uma ia makasua c Čp Čngaku.
S.PHON: apa ¶o Ēe nuu monakuin na Ēumo Ēuma ia makasua ¶…®p…®Ňčaku.
S.FORM: apaco'e nuu monakuin na 'umo'uma ia makasua c Čp Čngaku.
S.PHON: apa ¶o Ēe nuu monakuin na Ēumo Ēuma ia makasua ¶…®p…®Ňčaku.
S.TRANSL: Although I am in the field, what I think about is that way.

S.FORM: carenkiak Čc Č sii makasia 'akiacu mamarang.
S.PHON: ¶ar~…ĺenkiak…® ¶…® sii makasia Ēakia ¶u mamar~…ĺaŇč.
S.FORM: carenkiak Čc Č sii makasia 'akiacu mamarang.
S.PHON: ¶ar~…ĺenkiak…® ¶…® sii makasia Ēakia ¶u mamar~…ĺaŇč.

TRANSL.notes: I am poor and therefore (I should bear it), because my parents are already not (alive).

S.TRANSL: I am poor and therefore , because my parents are already not .

S.FORM: koocikia c Čm Čra cumaku koocikia c Čm Čra cinaamaku 'ecikia mamia par Čr Č' Č a.
S.PHON: koo ¶ikia ¶…®m…®r~…ĺa ¶umaku koo ¶ikia ¶…®m…®r~…ĺa ¶inaamaku Ēe ¶ikia mamia par~…ĺ…®r~…ĺ…® Ē…® a.
S.FORM: koocikia c Čm Čra cumaku koocikia c Čm Čra cinaamaku 'ecikia mamia par Čr Č' Č a.
S.PHON: koo ¶ikia ¶…®m…®r~…ĺa ¶umaku koo ¶ikia ¶…®m…®r~…ĺa ¶inaamaku Ēe ¶ikia mamia par~…ĺ…®r~…ĺ…® Ē…® a.

TRANSL.notes: I don't see my father, I don't see my mother, I can just only miss (them).

S.TRANSL: I don't see my father, I don't see my mother, I can just only miss .

S.FORM: nimikekiak Čc Č nanu mia a'aipikia misakia pac Čp Čc Čp Čng.
S.PHON: nimikekiak…® ¶…® nanu mia a Ēaipikia misakia pa ¶…®p…® ¶…®p…®Ňč.
S.FORM: nimikekiak Čc Č nanu mia a'aipikia misakia pac Čp Čc Čp Čng.
S.PHON: nimikekiak…® ¶…® nanu mia a Ēaipikia misakia pa ¶…®p…® ¶…®p…®Ňč.

TRANSL.notes: I keep think in this way, how (I have spent my life) since babyhood.

S.TRANSL: I keep think in this way, how since babyhood.

S.FORM: imo makasin ia ka'ancikia tavar Č' Č kavavantuku marangcikia.
S.PHON: imo makasin ia ka Ēan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kavavantuku mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikia.
S.FORM: imo makasin ia ka'ancikia tavar Č' Č kavavantuku marangcikia.
S.PHON: imo makasin ia ka Ēan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kavavantuku mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikia.
S.TRANSL: Therefore now I can't make money; I am old.

S.FORM: ka'ancikia tavar Č' Č kanupanupas Č marangcikia.
S.PHON: ka Ēan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kanupanupas…® mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikia.
S.FORM: ka'ancikia tavar Č' Č kanupanupas Č marangcikia.
S.PHON: ka Ēan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kanupanupas…® mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikia.
S.TRANSL: I can't make money; I am old.

S.FORM: neci'ire er sua Kanakanavu makasi masin ia er ma icuku mamia marang ta marang kis Č Čn.
S.PHON: ne ¶i Ēir~…ĺe er~…ĺ sua kanakanavu makasi masin ia er~…ĺ ma i ¶uku mamia mar~…ĺaŇč ta mar~…ĺaŇč kis…®…®n.
S.FORM: neci'ire er sua Kanakanavu makasi masin ia er ma icuku mamia marang ta marang kis Č Čn.
S.PHON: ne ¶i Ēir~…ĺe er~…ĺ sua kanakanavu makasi masin ia er~…ĺ ma i ¶uku mamia mar~…ĺaŇč ta mar~…ĺaŇč kis…®…®n.
S.TRANSL: You see,in Kanakanavu now only I am the so-called the eldest.

S.FORM: 'esi 'unaken tia sikaman Čng Kanakanavu kaisisi avara'e mamia ia manas Č pokaripa 'ikua.
S.PHON: Ēesi Ēunaken tia sikaman…®Ňč kanakanavu kaisisi avar~…ĺa Ēe mamia ia manas…® pokar~…ĺipa Ēikua.
S.FORM: 'esi 'unaken tia sikaman Čng Kanakanavu kaisisi avara'e mamia ia manas Č pokaripa 'ikua.
S.PHON: Ēesi Ēunaken tia sikaman…®Ňč kanakanavu kaisisi avar~…ĺa Ēe mamia ia manas…® pokar~…ĺipa Ēikua.

TRANSL.notes: As long as there are any Kanakanavu ceremonies, (they) are sure to invite me.

S.TRANSL: As long as there are any Kanakanavu ceremonies, are sure to invite me.

S.FORM: er 'inanu ikasu cuma Mu'u?
S.PHON: er~…ĺ Ēinanu ikasu ¶uma mu Ēu?
S.FORM: er 'inanu ikasu cuma Mu'u?
S.PHON: er~…ĺ Ēinanu ikasu ¶uma mu Ēu?

S.FORM: er ivata sian ikasu tia tapaipecaa kari sii.
S.PHON: er~…ĺ ivata sian ikasu tia tapaipe ¶aa kar~…ĺi sii.
S.FORM: er ivata sian ikasu tia tapaipecaa kari sii.
S.PHON: er~…ĺ ivata sian ikasu tia tapaipe ¶aa kar~…ĺi sii.

TRANSL.notes: You come here because (we) want talk through you (with the clansmen or ancestors).

S.TRANSL: You come here because want talk through you .

S.FORM: nakai isi ia maman Čng sii iku nuu 'apatanganaiku tamu kakangca tamvina' Č misee ia macangcangar Č c Čp Čngaku sii nec Č' Čre.
S.PHON: nakai isi ia maman…®Ňč sii iku nuu ĒapataŇčanaiku tamu kakaŇč ¶a tamvina Ē…® misee ia ma ¶aŇč ¶aŇčar~…ĺ…® ¶…®p…®Ňčaku sii ne ¶…® Ē…®r~…ĺe.
S.FORM: nakai isi ia maman Čng sii iku nuu 'apatanganaiku tamu kakangca tamvina' Č misee ia macangcangar Č c Čp Čngaku sii nec Č' Čre.
S.PHON: nakai isi ia maman…®Ňč sii iku nuu ĒapataŇčanaiku tamu kakaŇč ¶a tamvina Ē…® misee ia ma ¶aŇč ¶aŇčar~…ĺ…® ¶…®p…®Ňčaku sii ne ¶…® Ē…®r~…ĺe.

TRANSL.notes: But this is good, because if (our people) talk with the god and the god of millet through me, I will be happy in my mind.

S.TRANSL: But this is good, because if talk with the god and the god of millet through me, I will be happy in my mind.

S.FORM: makai kisaa mamarang miana mate'e kitana manu mamanu.
S.PHON: makai kisaa mamar~…ĺaŇč miana mate Ēe kitana manu mamanu.
S.FORM: makai kisaa mamarang miana mate'e kitana manu mamanu.
S.PHON: makai kisaa mamar~…ĺaŇč miana mate Ēe kitana manu mamanu.

TRANSL.notes: Like what the old people said in the past, (the tribal traditions) were passed down to us children.

S.TRANSL: Like what the old people said in the past, were passed down to us children.

S.FORM: ikam maman ia 'akun aka c Čp Čng.
S.PHON: ikam maman ia Ēakun aka ¶…®p…®Ňč.
S.FORM: ikam maman ia 'akun aka c Čp Čng.
S.PHON: ikam maman ia Ēakun aka ¶…®p…®Ňč.

S.FORM: caniara noo kumareekam vina' Č.
S.PHON: ¶aniar~…ĺa noo kumar~…ĺeekam vina Ē…®.
S.FORM: caniara noo kumareekam vina' Č.
S.PHON: ¶aniar~…ĺa noo kumar~…ĺeekam vina Ē…®.

S.FORM: nimerivina' Čcukamu ia sua vina' Č ia um Čk Č Čncukamu vian' Č ia.
S.PHON: nimer~…ĺivina Ē…® ¶ukamu ia sua vina Ē…® ia um…®k…®…®n ¶ukamu vian Ē…® ia.
S.FORM: nimerivina' Čcukamu ia sua vina' Č ia um Čk Č Čncukamu vian' Č ia.
S.PHON: nimer~…ĺivina Ē…® ¶ukamu ia sua vina Ē…® ia um…®k…®…®n ¶ukamu vian Ē…® ia.
S.TRANSL: You harvested millet, the millet‚Ä¶ you planted the millet ‚Ä¶

S.FORM: mak mac Čp Čc Čp Čngcu mamia manas Č tecukam mon na cak Čr Č kaisisi.
S.PHON: mak ma ¶…®p…® ¶…®p…®Ňč ¶u mamia manas…® te ¶ukam mon na ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…® kaisisi.
S.FORM: mak mac Čp Čc Čp Čngcu mamia manas Č tecukam mon na cak Čr Č kaisisi.
S.PHON: mak ma ¶…®p…® ¶…®p…®Ňč ¶u mamia manas…® te ¶ukam mon na ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…® kaisisi.
S.TRANSL: Only remember‚Ä¶You may be about to worship ancestors in the chamber.

S.FORM: mikoo kisaa mamarang sii 'ecu tia marivina' Č.
S.PHON: mikoo kisaa mamar~…ĺaŇč sii Ēe ¶u tia mar~…ĺivina Ē…®.
S.FORM: mikoo kisaa mamarang sii 'ecu tia marivina' Č.
S.PHON: mikoo kisaa mamar~…ĺaŇč sii Ēe ¶u tia mar~…ĺivina Ē…®.
S.TRANSL: What the elder call 'MIKOON', because we are already going to harvest the millet.

S.FORM: ka'anpa aka c Čp Čng sua isua.
S.PHON: ka Ēanpa aka ¶…®p…®Ňč sua isua.
S.FORM: ka'anpa aka c Čp Čng sua isua.
S.PHON: ka Ēanpa aka ¶…®p…®Ňč sua isua.
S.TRANSL: That, will not be sad yet.

S.FORM: imoo misaaku mamia sua ikita kanakanavu ia sua katatamu misee ia 'akun sua masi'upupang.
S.PHON: imoo misaaku mamia sua ikita kanakanavu ia sua katatamu misee ia Ēakun sua masi ĒupupaŇč.
S.FORM: imoo misaaku mamia sua ikita kanakanavu ia sua katatamu misee ia 'akun sua masi'upupang.
S.PHON: imoo misaaku mamia sua ikita kanakanavu ia sua katatamu misee ia Ēakun sua masi ĒupupaŇč.

TRANSL.notes: So I just say, we Kanakanavu people, the so-called libation, (one) cannot say (prayers) casually.

S.TRANSL: So I just say, we Kanakanavu people, the so-called libation, cannot say casually.

S.FORM: sua ka'an tavar Č' Č pokarikari ia 'akun mamia pokarikari.
S.PHON: sua ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® pokar~…ĺikar~…ĺi ia Ēakun mamia pokar~…ĺikar~…ĺi.
S.FORM: sua ka'an tavar Č' Č pokarikari ia 'akun mamia pokarikari.
S.PHON: sua ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® pokar~…ĺikar~…ĺi ia Ēakun mamia pokar~…ĺikar~…ĺi.
S.TRANSL: Those who don't know how to say, don't speak then.

S.FORM: tia kari ucanen maamia sua karimusu.
S.PHON: tia kar~…ĺi u ¶anen maamia sua kar~…ĺimusu.
S.FORM: tia kari ucanen maamia sua karimusu.
S.PHON: tia kar~…ĺi u ¶anen maamia sua kar~…ĺimusu.
S.TRANSL: What you will say is only one sentence.

S.FORM: tamoo amancukasu mamarang misee mamia 'akun sua 'una kari noo katatamukita noo 'unakiten kari aka ia sii sua tavara'a mamia.
S.PHON: tamoo aman ¶ukasu mamar~…ĺaŇč misee mamia Ēakun sua Ēuna kar~…ĺi noo katatamukita noo Ēunakiten kar~…ĺi aka ia sii sua tavar~…ĺa Ēa mamia.
S.FORM: tamoo amancukasu mamarang misee mamia 'akun sua 'una kari noo katatamukita noo 'unakiten kari aka ia sii sua tavara'a mamia.
S.PHON: tamoo aman ¶ukasu mamar~…ĺaŇč misee mamia Ēakun sua Ēuna kar~…ĺi noo katatamukita noo Ēunakiten kar~…ĺi aka ia sii sua tavar~…ĺa Ēa mamia.
S.TRANSL: Our ancestors! When we libate, if we have bad prayers.

S.FORM: nec Čre.
S.PHON: ne ¶…®r~…ĺe.
S.FORM: nec Čre.
S.PHON: ne ¶…®r~…ĺe.

S.FORM: noo tecikita kaisisi ia kan 'esicu tinpurupuru nia cak Čr Č via aririan kitana.
S.PHON: noo te ¶ikita kaisisi ia kan Ēesi ¶u tinpur~…ĺupur~…ĺu nia ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…® via ar~…ĺir~…ĺian kitana.
S.FORM: noo tecikita kaisisi ia kan 'esicu tinpurupuru nia cak Čr Č via aririan kitana.
S.PHON: noo te ¶ikita kaisisi ia kan Ēesi ¶u tinpur~…ĺupur~…ĺu nia ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…® via ar~…ĺir~…ĺian kitana.

TRANSL.notes: When we are going to worship, (all the people) are sitting there in the chamber.

S.TRANSL: When we are going to worship, are sitting there in the chamber.

S.FORM: AH makanan tia s tekita cumac Č' Čra mamanmita sikaisisi mamanmita makan mat Ča maka mataa sikaisisita mamarang.
S.PHON: ah makanan tia s tekita ¶uma ¶…® Ē…®r~…ĺa mamanmita sikaisisi mamanmita makan mat…®a maka mataa sikaisisita mamar~…ĺaŇč.
S.FORM: AH makanan tia s tekita cumac Č' Čra mamanmita sikaisisi mamanmita makan mat Ča maka mataa sikaisisita mamarang.
S.PHON: ah makanan tia s tekita ¶uma ¶…® Ē…®r~…ĺa mamanmita sikaisisi mamanmita makan mat…®a maka mataa sikaisisita mamar~…ĺaŇč.
S.TRANSL: How to worship ancestors ? We will see if the way our offspring worship is the same as the way the elderly did.

S.FORM: misee AH mamarangmita.
S.PHON: misee ah mamar~…ĺaŇčmita.
S.FORM: misee AH mamarangmita.
S.PHON: misee ah mamar~…ĺaŇčmita.
S.TRANSL: Our ancestors will say that, is it the same as the way our ancestors worshiped?

S.FORM: imooku misee sua mokusakita cak Čr Č ia 'akun masipupang.
S.PHON: imooku misee sua mokusakita ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…® ia Ēakun masipupaŇč.
S.FORM: imooku misee sua mokusakita cak Čr Č ia 'akun masipupang.
S.PHON: imooku misee sua mokusakita ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…® ia Ēakun masipupaŇč.

TRANSL.notes: So I say, when we go to the chamber, (we) do not speak casually.

S.TRANSL: So I say, when we go to the chamber, do not speak casually.

S.FORM: sua ka'an tavar Č' Č pokarikari ia tamoo kisaa mamia.
S.PHON: sua ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® pokar~…ĺikar~…ĺi ia tamoo kisaa mamia.
S.FORM: sua ka'an tavar Č' Č pokarikari ia tamoo kisaa mamia.
S.PHON: sua ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® pokar~…ĺikar~…ĺi ia tamoo kisaa mamia.
S.TRANSL: Those who don't know how to say just say 'tamoo'.

S.FORM: nguaci sua kari hua.
S.PHON: Ňčua ¶i sua kar~…ĺi hua.
S.FORM: nguaci sua kari hua.
S.PHON: Ňčua ¶i sua kar~…ĺi hua.

S.FORM: nec Čre kan 'unakita 'e na Kanakanavu p Č' Čp Č kanakanavu i masiupupang noo katatamukita cak Čr Č.
S.PHON: ne ¶…®r~…ĺe kan Ēunakita Ēe na kanakanavu p…® Ē…®p…® kanakanavu i masiupupaŇč noo katatamukita ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…®.
S.FORM: nec Čre kan 'unakita 'e na Kanakanavu p Č' Čp Č kanakanavu i masiupupang noo katatamukita cak Čr Č.
S.PHON: ne ¶…®r~…ĺe kan Ēunakita Ēe na kanakanavu p…® Ē…®p…® kanakanavu i masiupupaŇč noo katatamukita ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…®.

TRANSL.notes: You see, we have in‚Ä¶ we the remaining Kanakanavu people, when we libate in the chamber, (we) say the prayers casually‚Ä¶

S.TRANSL: You see, we have in‚Ä¶ we the remaining Kanakanavu people, when we libate in the chamber, say the prayers casually‚Ä¶

S.FORM: nec Č' Čre 'unakita araaka.
S.PHON: ne ¶…® Ē…®r~…ĺe Ēunakita ar~…ĺaaka.
S.FORM: nec Č' Čre 'unakita araaka.
S.PHON: ne ¶…® Ē…®r~…ĺe Ēunakita ar~…ĺaaka.
S.TRANSL: You see, we have some people who passed away.

S.FORM: m a Č 'aka sua tina'anin.
S.PHON: m a…® Ēaka sua tina Ēanin.
S.FORM: m a Č 'aka sua tina'anin.
S.PHON: m a…® Ēaka sua tina Ēanin.
S.TRANSL: Some are in bad physical condition.

S.FORM: ka'an maman Čng si'ic Čpin.
S.PHON: ka Ēan maman…®Ňč si Ēi ¶…®pin.
S.FORM: ka'an maman Čng si'ic Čpin.
S.PHON: ka Ēan maman…®Ňč si Ēi ¶…®pin.

TRANSL.notes: He does not sleep well. (MET. He has bad luck)

S.FORM: sip Čc Čc Čpin sua makasua sua.
S.PHON: sip…® ¶…® ¶…®pin sua makasua sua.
S.FORM: sip Čc Čc Čpin sua makasua sua.
S.PHON: sip…® ¶…® ¶…®pin sua makasua sua.

S.FORM: oh ka'an masiman Čng Čn mon na cak Čr Č.
S.PHON: oh ka Ēan masiman…®Ňč…®n mon na ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…®.
S.FORM: oh ka'an masiman Čng Čn mon na cak Čr Č.
S.PHON: oh ka Ēan masiman…®Ňč…®n mon na ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…®.
S.TRANSL: The prayers are not said in the right way in the chamber.

S.FORM: imoo pairaku makasua noo 'unakuin.
S.PHON: imoo pair~…ĺaku makasua noo Ēunakuin.
S.FORM: imoo pairaku makasua noo 'unakuin.
S.PHON: imoo pair~…ĺaku makasua noo Ēunakuin.
S.TRANSL: So I'm often like that, if I have time.

S.FORM: noo kaisisikita ia matacuvucuvungkita ia manas Čku paira pu'una kari kamua.
S.PHON: noo kaisisikita ia mata ¶uvu ¶uvuŇčkita ia manas…®ku pair~…ĺa pu Ēuna kar~…ĺi kamua.
S.FORM: noo kaisisikita ia matacuvucuvungkita ia manas Čku paira pu'una kari kamua.
S.PHON: noo kaisisikita ia mata ¶uvu ¶uvuŇčkita ia manas…®ku pair~…ĺa pu Ēuna kar~…ĺi kamua.
S.TRANSL: When we worship, we gather together and I often will something to tell you first.

S.FORM: aah mamanu 'akunen masipupang katatamukamu.
S.PHON: aah mamanu Ēakunen masipupaŇč katatamukamu.
S.FORM: aah mamanu 'akunen masipupang katatamukamu.
S.PHON: aah mamanu Ēakunen masipupaŇč katatamukamu.
S.TRANSL: Ah, children! Don't say the prayers casually when you libate.

S.FORM: masiman Čnga sua ka'an tavar Č' Č pokarikari ia 'akun maa pokarikari misaaku.
S.PHON: masiman…®Ňča sua ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® pokar~…ĺikar~…ĺi ia Ēakun maa pokar~…ĺikar~…ĺi misaaku.
S.FORM: masiman Čnga sua ka'an tavar Č' Č pokarikari ia 'akun maa pokarikari misaaku.
S.PHON: masiman…®Ňča sua ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® pokar~…ĺikar~…ĺi ia Ēakun maa pokar~…ĺikar~…ĺi misaaku.
S.TRANSL: Those who don't know how to say don't speak then, I say.

S.FORM: mataa ucani ia.
S.PHON: mataa u ¶ani ia.
S.FORM: mataa ucani ia.
S.PHON: mataa u ¶ani ia.

S.FORM: caniava noo makai mikong kita kaisisi ia cucuru makai uh ak sua ikita mamisa ia 'esicikita mamia umara mamia nganee seehu seehu miseekita mamia.
S.PHON: ¶aniava noo makai mikoŇč kita kaisisi ia ¶u ¶ur~…ĺu makai uh ak sua ikita mamisa ia Ēesi ¶ikita mamia umar~…ĺa mamia Ňčanee seehu seehu miseekita mamia.
S.FORM: caniava noo makai mikong kita kaisisi ia cucuru makai uh ak sua ikita mamisa ia 'esicikita mamia umara mamia nganee seehu seehu miseekita mamia.
S.PHON: ¶aniava noo makai mikoŇč kita kaisisi ia ¶u ¶ur~…ĺu makai uh ak sua ikita mamisa ia Ēesi ¶ikita mamia umar~…ĺa mamia Ňčanee seehu seehu miseekita mamia.
S.TRANSL: Like Mikong, when we have the Mikong Festival, we only say that recently we celebrate it merely for the sake of the government.

S.FORM: cucuru sua mamarang mita miana ia ka'an tavar Č' Č 'apac Č' Čra pakisia noo kaisisi.
S.PHON: ¶u ¶ur~…ĺu sua mamar~…ĺaŇč mita miana ia ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® Ēapa ¶…® Ē…®r~…ĺa pakisia noo kaisisi.
S.FORM: cucuru sua mamarang mita miana ia ka'an tavar Č' Č 'apac Č' Čra pakisia noo kaisisi.
S.PHON: ¶u ¶ur~…ĺu sua mamar~…ĺaŇč mita miana ia ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® Ēapa ¶…® Ē…®r~…ĺa pakisia noo kaisisi.

TRANSL.notes: Our ancestors indeed would not let Taiwanese-speaking people see (Mikong) when worshiping.

S.TRANSL: Our ancestors indeed would not let Taiwanese-speaking people see when worshiping.

S.FORM: ka'an tavar Č' Č 'apivatu na tapininga cak Čr Č.
S.PHON: ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® Ēapivatu na tapiniŇča ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…®.
S.FORM: ka'an tavar Č' Č 'apivatu na tapininga cak Čr Č.
S.PHON: ka Ēan tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® Ēapivatu na tapiniŇča ¶ak…®r~…ĺ…®.

S.FORM: uh matarava mamia noo nikaisisicikita makai vina' Č.
S.PHON: uh matar~…ĺava mamia noo nikaisisi ¶ikita makai vina Ē…®.
S.FORM: uh matarava mamia noo nikaisisicikita makai vina' Č.
S.PHON: uh matar~…ĺava mamia noo nikaisisi ¶ikita makai vina Ē…®.
S.TRANSL: Only when we finish worshiping, like millet.

S.FORM: uh tumin coca'ania ia tavar Č' Čcikita pakisia makari ah tekamu iovutu patara'aningo ia ivataacu.
S.PHON: uh tumin ¶o ¶a Ēania ia tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® ¶ikita pakisia makar~…ĺi ah tekamu iovutu patar~…ĺa ĒaniŇčo ia ivataa ¶u.
S.FORM: uh tumin coca'ania ia tavar Č' Čcikita pakisia makari ah tekamu iovutu patara'aningo ia ivataacu.
S.PHON: uh tumin ¶o ¶a Ēania ia tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® ¶ikita pakisia makar~…ĺi ah tekamu iovutu patar~…ĺa ĒaniŇčo ia ivataa ¶u.
S.TRANSL: If we finish hanging up the lunch, we can tell the Chinese people: 'If you want to come, now you can.'

S.FORM: tavar Č' Čn makasua pakisia.
S.PHON: tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…®n makasua pakisia.
S.FORM: tavar Č' Čn makasua pakisia.
S.PHON: tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…®n makasua pakisia.
S.TRANSL: We can treat the Chinese-speaking people that way.

S.FORM: imo ikita Kanakanavu kan sikaisisimita ia aka makaa maraa aka sii.
S.PHON: imo ikita kanakanavu kan sikaisisimita ia aka makaa mar~…ĺaa aka sii.
S.FORM: imo ikita Kanakanavu kan sikaisisimita ia aka makaa maraa aka sii.
S.PHON: imo ikita kanakanavu kan sikaisisimita ia aka makaa mar~…ĺaa aka sii.
S.TRANSL: So the way we worship is not good, is worse, because‚Ä¶

S.FORM: iavutucu ma kitana pakisia uh arat Čm arat Čm arat Čm mamia.
S.PHON: iavutu ¶u ma kitana pakisia uh ar~…ĺat…®m ar~…ĺat…®m ar~…ĺat…®m mamia.
S.FORM: iavutucu ma kitana pakisia uh arat Čm arat Čm arat Čm mamia.
S.PHON: iavutu ¶u ma kitana pakisia uh ar~…ĺat…®m ar~…ĺat…®m ar~…ĺat…®m mamia.
S.TRANSL: The Chinese people came straight to our place, and they just showed up.

S.FORM: ka'ancikita tavar Č' Č kaisisi.
S.PHON: ka Ēan ¶ikita tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kaisisi.
S.FORM: ka'ancikita tavar Č' Č kaisisi.
S.PHON: ka Ēan ¶ikita tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kaisisi.

S.FORM: isua sua kan hayab m maka hayab.
S.PHON: isua sua kan haja* m maka haja*.
S.FORM: isua sua kan hayab m maka hayab.
S.PHON: isua sua kan haja* m maka haja*.

S.FORM: makanan kusai secu'urakitana tamu kakangca tamvina' Č.
S.PHON: makanan kusai se ¶u Ēur~…ĺakitana tamu kakaŇč ¶a tamvina Ē…®.
S.FORM: makanan kusai secu'urakitana tamu kakangca tamvina' Č.
S.PHON: makanan kusai se ¶u Ēur~…ĺakitana tamu kakaŇč ¶a tamvina Ē…®.
S.TRANSL: How exactly would our ancestors and the god of millet see us?

S.FORM: sii makasuakita 'esipikita mamia tia matacuvucuvung makarikari.
S.PHON: sii makasuakita Ēesipikita mamia tia mata ¶uvu ¶uvuŇč makar~…ĺikar~…ĺi.
S.FORM: sii makasuakita 'esipikita mamia tia matacuvucuvung makarikari.
S.PHON: sii makasuakita Ēesipikita mamia tia mata ¶uvu ¶uvuŇč makar~…ĺikar~…ĺi.
S.TRANSL: Because we are like that, we're still gathering together, discussing.

S.FORM: 'esici nia pakisia kuratumuru mamia.
S.PHON: Ēesi ¶i nia pakisia kur~…ĺatumur~…ĺu mamia.
S.FORM: 'esici nia pakisia kuratumuru mamia.
S.PHON: Ēesi ¶i nia pakisia kur~…ĺatumur~…ĺu mamia.
S.TRANSL: The Chinese people were just everywhere.

S.FORM: tekita manan mangarigari.
S.PHON: tekita manan maŇčar~…ĺi*ar~…ĺi.
S.FORM: tekita manan mangarigari.
S.PHON: tekita manan maŇčar~…ĺi*ar~…ĺi.

S.FORM: sii sua taniara soni ia 'esiku sian.

S.FORM: 'esiku pokarikari.

S.FORM: 'esiku pokarikari.

S.FORM: po' Č' Čna po'isua sua uh tanasamaku sii.

S.FORM: po' Č' Čna po'isua sua uh tanasamaku sii.
S.PHON: po Ē…® Ē…®na po Ēisua sua uh tanasamaku sii.
S.TRANSL: Tell stories about the‚Ä¶ my home, because‚Ä¶

S.FORM: aaka sua aakaci c Čp Čngaku sii marangcikia.
S.PHON: aaka sua aaka ¶i ¶…®p…®Ňčaku sii mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikia.
S.FORM: aaka sua aakaci c Čp Čngaku sii marangcikia.
S.PHON: aaka sua aaka ¶i ¶…®p…®Ňčaku sii mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikia.
S.TRANSL: I already feel sad because‚Ä¶I become old.

S.FORM: kancikia tavar Č' Č kanupanupas Č.
S.PHON: kan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kanupanupas…®.
S.FORM: kancikia tavar Č' Č kanupanupas Č.
S.PHON: kan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kanupanupas…®.
S.TRANSL: I'm already unable to earn money.

S.FORM: akiacu k Čna kam Čc Č.
S.PHON: akia ¶u k…®na kam…® ¶…®.
S.FORM: akiacu k Čna kam Čc Č.

S.FORM: v Čra.
S.PHON: v…®r~…ĺa.
S.FORM: v Čra.

S.FORM: kani 'ecikia mamia purus Čs Č' Č mamia taniara.

S.PHON: kani Ēe ¶ikia mamia pur~…ĺus…®s…® Ē…® mamia taniar~…ĺa.

S.FORM: paira aaka c Čp Čngaku sii iki mata miciku ia marangcikimi.
S.PHON: pair~…ĺa aaka ¶…®p…®Ňčaku sii iki mata mi ¶iku ia mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikimi.
S.FORM: paira aaka c Čp Čngaku sii iki mata miciku ia marangcikimi.
S.PHON: pair~…ĺa aaka ¶…®p…®Ňčaku sii iki mata mi ¶iku ia mar~…ĺaŇč ¶ikimi.
S.TRANSL: I often feel sad because I and Miciku have become old.

S.FORM: ka'ancikia tavar Č putukikio.
S.PHON: ka Ēan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…® putukikio.
S.FORM: ka'ancikia tavar Č putukikio.
S.PHON: ka Ēan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…® putukikio.

S.FORM: 'unakim manu misaakam cau nakai.
S.PHON: Ēunakim manu misaakam ¶au nakai.
S.FORM: 'unakim manu misaakam cau nakai.
S.PHON: Ēunakim manu misaakam ¶au nakai.
S.TRANSL: You say that I'm one who has a child, but‚Ä¶

S.FORM: sua manumaku ia uh takacicin kangvang 'esi namanamar Č putikikio namanamar Č.
S.PHON: sua manumaku ia uh taka ¶i ¶in kaŇčvaŇč Ēesi namanamar~…ĺ…® putikikio namanamar~…ĺ…®.
S.FORM: sua manumaku ia uh takacicin kangvang 'esi namanamar Č putikikio namanamar Č.
S.PHON: sua manumaku ia uh taka ¶i ¶in kaŇčvaŇč Ēesi namanamar~…ĺ…® putikikio namanamar~…ĺ…®.
S.TRANSL: But my children are all working in the city.

S.FORM: tekam makasua sua sia situturuamaku kamua.
S.PHON: tekam makasua sua sia situtur~…ĺuamaku kamua.
S.FORM: tekam makasua sua sia situturuamaku kamua.
S.PHON: tekam makasua sua sia situtur~…ĺuamaku kamua.
S.TRANSL: I use that to tell you.

S.FORM: noo tumanakamu kari iisi ia sua kanang ia kan mana tia paiee.
S.PHON: noo tumanakamu kar~…ĺi iisi ia sua kanaŇč ia kan mana tia paiee.
S.FORM: noo tumanakamu kari iisi ia sua kanang ia kan mana tia paiee.
S.PHON: noo tumanakamu kar~…ĺi iisi ia sua kanaŇč ia kan mana tia paiee.
S.TRANSL: If you hear these words, those who understand will probably regret.

S.FORM: ah cucuruana Cuma Mu'u ia kari Cuma Mu'u ia 'esi poman Čng Č.
S.PHON: ah ¶u ¶ur~…ĺuana ¶uma mu Ēu ia kar~…ĺi ¶uma mu Ēu ia Ēesi poman…®Ňč…®.
S.FORM: ah cucuruana Cuma Mu'u ia kari Cuma Mu'u ia 'esi poman Čng Č.
S.PHON: ah ¶u ¶ur~…ĺuana ¶uma mu Ēu ia kar~…ĺi ¶uma mu Ēu ia Ēesi poman…®Ňč…®.
S.TRANSL: Uncle Mu'u's words are really clear.

S.FORM: caren curu sii marangcu.
S.PHON: ¶ar~…ĺen ¶ur~…ĺu sii mar~…ĺaŇč ¶u.
S.FORM: caren curu sii marangcu.
S.PHON: ¶ar~…ĺen ¶ur~…ĺu sii mar~…ĺaŇč ¶u.
S.TRANSL: It's really pathetic, because I'm old.

S.FORM: kancikia tavar Ču kanupanupas Č.
S.PHON: kan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…®u kanupanupas…®.
S.FORM: kancikia tavar Ču kanupanupas Č.
S.PHON: kan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…®u kanupanupas…®.
S.TRANSL: I'm already unable to make money.

S.FORM: kancikia tavar Č' Č kipapa cau kanupanupas Č.
S.PHON: kan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kipapa ¶au kanupanupas…®.
S.FORM: kancikia tavar Č' Č kipapa cau kanupanupas Č.
S.PHON: kan ¶ikia tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kipapa ¶au kanupanupas…®.
S.TRANSL: I'm already unable to follow people and make money.

S.FORM: 'esicikia mamia tinpuru mamia par Čr Č' Č mamia.
S.PHON: Ēesi ¶ikia mamia tinpur~…ĺu mamia par~…ĺ…®r~…ĺ…® Ē…® mamia.
S.FORM: 'esicikia mamia tinpuru mamia par Čr Č' Č mamia.
S.PHON: Ēesi ¶ikia mamia tinpur~…ĺu mamia par~…ĺ…®r~…ĺ…® Ē…® mamia.

TRANSL.notes: We just sit here, and recollect (the past).

S.TRANSL: We just sit here, and recollect .

S.FORM: tecikiei mosa makananu.
S.PHON: te ¶ikiei mosa makananu.
S.FORM: tecikiei mosa makananu.
S.PHON: te ¶ikiei mosa makananu.

S.FORM: ka'ancikia sipisipi taniara ia tecikiei mosa makananu manmanmaku.
S.PHON: ka Ēan ¶ikia sipisipi taniar~…ĺa ia te ¶ikiei mosa makananu manmanmaku.
S.FORM: ka'ancikia sipisipi taniara ia tecikiei mosa makananu manmanmaku.
S.PHON: ka Ēan ¶ikia sipisipi taniar~…ĺa ia te ¶ikiei mosa makananu manmanmaku.
S.TRANSL: My days are not SIPISIPI, then what exactly should our children do?

S.FORM: pac Čp Čngc Čp Čng misaaku mamia pac Čp Čngc Čp Čng sua karimaku tumaturu kamua ia manas Č matarava mamia nesi.
S.PHON: pa ¶…®p…®Ňč ¶…®p…®Ňč misaaku mamia pa ¶…®p…®Ňč ¶…®p…®Ňč sua kar~…ĺimaku tumatur~…ĺu kamua ia manas…® matar~…ĺava mamia nesi.
S.FORM: pac Čp Čngc Čp Čng misaaku mamia pac Čp Čngc Čp Čng sua karimaku tumaturu kamua ia manas Č matarava mamia nesi.
S.PHON: pa ¶…®p…®Ňč ¶…®p…®Ňč misaaku mamia pa ¶…®p…®Ňč ¶…®p…®Ňč sua kar~…ĺimaku tumatur~…ĺu kamua ia manas…® matar~…ĺava mamia nesi.
S.TRANSL: I think, what I want to tell you ends here.

S.FORM: pac Čp Čngc Čp Čngo makas(ua) sua na va sua kariin.
S.PHON: pa ¶…®p…®Ňč ¶…®p…®Ňčo makas(ua) sua na va sua kar~…ĺiin.
S.FORM: pac Čp Čngc Čp Čngo makas(ua) sua na va sua kariin.
S.PHON: pa ¶…®p…®Ňč ¶…®p…®Ňčo makas(ua) sua na va sua kar~…ĺiin.
S.TRANSL: Remember that, his words are like that.

S.FORM: Cuma Mu'u misaa manasi tavar Č' Č kamu pac Čp Čngc Čp Čng nuari.
S.PHON: ¶uma mu Ēu misaa manasi tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kamu pa ¶…®p…®Ňč ¶…®p…®Ňč nuar~…ĺi.
S.FORM: Cuma Mu'u misaa manasi tavar Č' Č kamu pac Čp Čngc Čp Čng nuari.
S.PHON: ¶uma mu Ēu misaa manasi tavar~…ĺ…® Ē…® kamu pa ¶…®p…®Ňč ¶…®p…®Ňč nuar~…ĺi.
S.TRANSL: Then you can understand the words Uncle Mu'u said in the future.
